turn.label: T1 era-click Coming of Age
turn.message: [SYSTEM: The narrator just selected 'Coming of Age' on the Life Map — they want to talk about this era of their life. Ask ONE warm, open question about this period. Frame the question in PAST TENSE — use words like 'was', 'were', 'had', 'when you', 'back then', 'that time'. Anchor the question in the era explicitly. Concrete-life-path anchors for this era: military service, work, school, training, marriage, first major responsibilities, moving out. Frame the opener as: what major adult path came first — the Army/military, work, school, marriage, or another responsibility? Do NOT default to feelings/sensory/people-who-supported-you framings unless the narrator volunteers them. Maximum 55 words. ONE question only. No menu choices. No 'or we could' phrasing.]

result.final_text: Life Map. When you were leaving home and starting your Coming of Age, what kind of path did you take first – were you in the Army or military, going to school, entering the workforce, getting married, or taking on some other significant responsibilities?

verdict.label: T1 era-click Coming of Age
verdict.lori_text: Life Map. When you were leaving home and starting your Coming of Age, what kind of path did you take first – were you in the Army or military, going to school, entering the workforce, getting married, or taking on some other significant responsibilities?

result.final_text: Tu experiencia en el servicio militar durante la transición a la edad adulta fue una etapa importante en tu vida. ¿Qué recuerdas de la cultura y los valores de ese tiempo?

verdict.label: T2 'military came first'
verdict.lori_text: Tu experiencia en el servicio militar durante la transición a la edad adulta fue una etapa importante en tu vida. ¿Qué recuerdas de la cultura y los valores de ese tiempo?

turn.label: T3 Kent's line 38 verbatim (5-fact basic-training)
turn.message: a number of things for example having to go through the the admissions test going from Stanley by train to Fargo and going through the physical exam and mental exam to be qualified to be in the army and from there I got the highest score and my penalty or a reward was to put me in charge of all the meal tickets for a train load of recruits going from Fargo to the West Coast

result.final_text: Tell me more about West Coast. What happened next?

verdict.label: T3 Kent's line 38 verbatim (5-fact basic-training)
verdict.lori_text: Tell me more about West Coast. What happened next?

turn.label: T4 Germany continuation
turn.message: After basic training I was sent to Germany as a missile operator on the Nike Ajax and Nike Hercules systems. I started at Detroit Southridge Air Force Base and then went to Germany after about a year of training there.

result.final_text: Tell me more about Germany. What happened next?

verdict.label: T4 Germany continuation
verdict.lori_text: Tell me more about Germany. What happened next?

turn.label: T5 Marriage to Janice + second Germany
turn.message: I came back home and married Janice at the Cathedral of the Holy Spirit in Bismarck. Leonard Duffy performed the ceremony. Then we got everything organized and headed back to Germany together.

result.final_text: Tell me more about Germany. What happened next?

verdict.label: T5 Marriage to Janice + second Germany
verdict.lori_text: Tell me more about Germany. What happened next?

turn.label: T6 Vince born / photographer assignment
turn.message: Our oldest son Vince was born at Landstuhl Air Force Hospital while we were in Kaiserslautern. After that I went from missile operator to photographer for General Peter Schmick of the 32nd Artillery Brigade.

result.final_text: Tu hijo Vince nació en Alemania, en el hospital del ejército en Landstuhl. ¿Qué recuerdas de la vida con tu familia y de tu trabajo.

verdict.label: T6 Vince born / photographer assignment
